steps.title: Identify which gene drives A. sicerae survival
steps.content: Type A flies (with DptA silenced) maintain ~95% survival throughout A. sicerae infection, while Type B flies (with DptB silenced) plummet to nearly 0% by day 10. This survival divergence proves that DptB, not DptA, is essential for A. sicerae defense.

steps.title: Match DptB's role to the answer choice
steps.content:
Choice B asserts that DptB may have developed as a specific defense against A. sicerae.

The survival curves support that claim: Type A flies, with DptA silenced but DptB intact, remain at high survival, while Type B flies, with DptB silenced, drop to nearly 0% survival by day 10.

A and C overstate DptA's role for A. sicerae, and D claims both genes together are strongest even though the graph isolates DptB as the key defense in this infection.

That confirms B is correct.
